Then Fiend Fire (2E): exhausts remaining hand (Strike, Strike = 2 cards). Damage = (7+5)*2 = 24 to one target. FNP: 2 exhausts * 3 = 6 block. Total block from FNP: 3+6=9. Hmm, that only hits one target for 24. Not great for evening out. Alternative: Spot Weakness on D0 (1E, +3 Str -> Str 5). Intimidate (0E, exhaust, Weak 1 all, FNP 3 block). Then I have 4E left. Wait, if I play all other cards first, FF has nothing to exhaust. FF base damage = 7+5=12 to target, 0 exhausts. Thats terrible. Better plan: Spot Weakness on D0 (1E, Str->5). 4E left. Strike on D1 (1E): 11*1.5(Vuln2)=16. D1: 50-16=34. 3E left. Strike on D2 (1E): 11 damage. D2: 30-11=19. 2E left. Now hand: Fiend Fire, Intimidate. If I play Intimidate (0E, exhaust, FNP 3 block, Weak all), then FF has 0 cards to exhaust. If I play FF (2E) on D1: exhausts Intimidate from hand = 1 exhaust = 12 damage + FNP 3 block. Net damage: 12-3=9. 51-9=42 HP. HP spread after: D0: 11(+12block), D1: 16, D2: 19. Thats much more even! Next turn Str 4 from Demon Form. But D0 still has 12 block + 11 HP = 23 effective. Actually wait - D0 block expires next turn. So next turn D0 is just 11 HP. BETTER PLAN: What if I use Fiend Fire on D1 BEFORE playing other cards to maximize exhausts? FF with 4 other cards: (7+2Str before SW)*4=36. No wait, I should still play SW first. SW(1E)->Str5. Then FF(2E) on D1 with 3 remaining cards (Intimidate, Strike, Strike): (7+5)*3 = 36 damage. D1 Vuln2: 36*1.5=54! D1: 50-54=DEAD? No wait, D1 has 50 HP and Im doing 54. D1 dies. FNP: 3 exhausts * 3 = 9 block. But Life Link! D1 dies alone, revives to 28 HP. Hmm. Actually maybe thats OK? If D1 revives to 28, then HP spread is D0:11, D1:28, D2:30. Pretty even! And I get 9 block. But the Intimidate exhaust means I lose it. And I lose both Strikes. Next turn draw pile has 3 Defend + Double Tap. With only those cards and whatever cycles back... Actually losing Fiend Fire itself to exhaust pile plus the 3 cards it exhausts leaves me with very few cards. Let me reconsider. Fiend Fire goes to exhaust after play. So exhaust pile: Intimidate, Reaper, Shockwave, + Intimidate#2, Strike, Strike, Fiend Fire = 7 exhausted. Remaining deck: Heavy Blade+, Carnage, Double Tap, Cleave, Thunderclap+, Bash, Armaments+, Defend x4, Strike x1, Spot Weakness. Thats still 12 cards. OK. So: SW(1E)->Str5. FF(2E) on D1: exhausts Intimidate+Strike+Strike = 3 cards. Damage = 12*3=36, *1.5 Vuln = 54. D1 dies, revives to 28. FNP: 3*3=9 block. Total: 9 block vs incoming 17. Net: 8 damage. 51-8=43 HP. HP spread: D0:11, D1:28, D2:30. Very even! This is the play. Then next turn Str 4, can burst them down together.

After Cleave: D0 dead, D1: 11, D2: 13. Then HB+ on one: 14+9*5=14+45=59 damage. Kills either easily. Then Strike on the other: 6+9+3(StrikeDummy)=18. D1: 11-18=DEAD. D2: 13-18=DEAD. If D0 dies from Cleave before the others die, does it trigger revive? Actually Cleave hits all simultaneously. D0 dies. Then I play HB+ and Strike to kill D1 and D2 on subsequent plays. But D0 is already dead from Cleave - will it revive before I kill the others? I think Life Link triggers at end of turn, not immediately. Actually looking at the mechanic: Life Link -1 means the Darkling lost its Life Link (it already used it). Wait, D1 shows Life Link -1 and it revived. D0 also shows Life Link -1. Does -1 mean it CAN still revive once? Or did it already use its revive? Looking at D1: it was killed by Fiend Fire last turn and revived this turn. Its Life Link is -1. D0 never died yet but also shows -1. So -1 might just mean the mechanic is active. OK, so the safe play: I need to kill all 3 in the same action or ensure they all die before end of turn. If I Cleave killing D0, then kill D1 with HB+ and D2 with Strike (or vice versa), all 3 are dead before turn ends. Life Link checks at end of turn - if all are dead, none revive. Actually lets check: can D1 revive again? It already revived once. In StS, each Darkling can revive once. Since D1 already revived, it cannot revive again (Life Link -1 = used up). D0 has Life Link -1 too but never died... hmm. Let me just play it safe and try to kill all 3. Cleave(1E): 17 AOE. D0: 11-17 = DEAD. D1: 28-17 = 11. D2: 30-17 = 13. HB+(2E) on D2: 59 damage -> DEAD. Strike(1E) on D1: 18 damage -> DEAD. Total: 4E. 1E left for Defend(5 block). But if all 3 die, combat ends and block doesnt matter. Lets do it! Wait, I should also consider: what if D0 can revive? If Life Link allows one revive per Darkling, and D0 hasnt died yet, killing it with Cleave might trigger a revive. But if D1 and D2 are already dead (they used their revive), D0 would be the only one alive and Life Link wouldnt trigger (all need to be dead for no revive, or... actually Life Link means: when this dies, if any other Darklings are alive, this revives). So if D0 dies from Cleave, D1 and D2 are still alive -> D0 would revive. BUT then I kill D1 and D2 afterward. D0 revived at half HP = 24. Then D1 dies (HB+), D2 dies (Strike). Now only D0 alive at 24 HP. D1 already used its revive so it stays dead. D2 never died before... wait D2 hasnt died yet. Hmm this is getting complicated. Actually let me just try a different approach: dont use AOE. Use targeted damage to bring all 3 to killable range, then finish with Cleave AOE. HB+(2E) on D2: 59 damage -> D2 dead (30 HP). Strike(1E) on D1: 18 damage -> D1 goes to 28-18=10. Strike(1E) on D0: 18 damage -> D0 goes to 11-18=DEAD. Now D0 and D2 dead, D1 at 10. Life Link: D0 revives (D1 still alive), D2 revives (D1 still alive). Bad! OK the CORRECT approach: bring all to Cleave kill range first, then Cleave to kill all simultaneously. D0: 11 HP, D1: 28, D2: 30. Cleave does 17. To kill all with Cleave, all need to be at 17 or less. D0 already at 11 (below 17). D1 needs to go from 28 to 17 or less = need 11+ damage. D2 needs to go from 30 to 17 or less = need 13+ damage. Strike does 18 on either. HB+ does 59. Plan: Strike(1E) on D1: 18 damage -> D1 goes to 10. Strike(1E) on D2: 18 damage -> D2 goes to 12. Cleave(1E): 17 AOE. D0: 11-17=DEAD. D1: 10-17=DEAD. D2: 12-17=DEAD. All die simultaneously! 3E total. I have 5E. 2E left for Defend and HB+ but no need. THIS IS THE PLAY.

If I Cleave killing D0, then kill D1 with HB+ and D2 with Strike (or vice versa), all 3 are dead before turn ends. Life Link checks at end of turn - if all are dead, none revive. Actually lets check: can D1 revive again? It already revived once. In StS, each Darkling can revive once. Since D1 already revived, it cannot revive again (Life Link -1 = used up). D0 has Life Link -1 too but never died... hmm. Let me just play it safe and try to kill all 3. Cleave(1E): 17 AOE. D0: 11-17 = DEAD. D1: 28-17 = 11. D2: 30-17 = 13. HB+(2E) on D2: 59 damage -> DEAD. Strike(1E) on D1: 18 damage -> DEAD. Total: 4E. 1E left for Defend(5 block). But if all 3 die, combat ends and block doesnt matter. Lets do it! Wait, I should also consider: what if D0 can revive? If Life Link allows one revive per Darkling, and D0 hasnt died yet, killing it with Cleave might trigger a revive. But if D1 and D2 are already dead (they used their revive), D0 would be the only one alive and Life Link wouldnt trigger (all need to be dead for no revive, or... actually Life Link means: when this dies, if any other Darklings are alive, this revives). So if D0 dies from Cleave, D1 and D2 are still alive -> D0 would revive. BUT then I kill D1 and D2 afterward. D0 revived at half HP = 24. Then D1 dies (HB+), D2 dies (Strike). Now only D0 alive at 24 HP. D1 already used its revive so it stays dead. D2 never died before... wait D2 hasnt died yet. Hmm this is getting complicated. Actually let me just try a different approach: dont use AOE. Use targeted damage to bring all 3 to killable range, then finish with Cleave AOE. HB+(2E) on D2: 59 damage -> D2 dead (30 HP). Strike(1E) on D1: 18 damage -> D1 goes to 28-18=10. Strike(1E) on D0: 18 damage -> D0 goes to 11-18=DEAD. Now D0 and D2 dead, D1 at 10. Life Link: D0 revives (D1 still alive), D2 revives (D1 still alive). Bad! OK the CORRECT approach: bring all to Cleave kill range first, then Cleave to kill all simultaneously. D0: 11 HP, D1: 28, D2: 30. Cleave does 17. To kill all with Cleave, all need to be at 17 or less. D0 already at 11 (below 17). D1 needs to go from 28 to 17 or less = need 11+ damage. D2 needs to go from 30 to 17 or less = need 13+ damage. Strike does 18 on either. HB+ does 59. Plan: Strike(1E) on D1: 18 damage -> D1 goes to 10. Strike(1E) on D2: 18 damage -> D2 goes to 12. Cleave(1E): 17 AOE. D0: 11-17=DEAD. D1: 10-17=DEAD. D2: 12-17=DEAD. All die simultaneously! 3E total. I have 5E. 2E left for Defend and HB+ but no need. THIS IS THE PLAY.

Hand: FNP(1E power), Bash(2E), Demon Form(3E power), Intimidate(0E exhaust), Reaper(2E exhaust). Hallway fight so I should NOT play Powers (waste in a hallway and Demon Form is 3E). But Thorns 7 means I take 7 HP from Reapers hit. 4 healing per enemy - 7 thorns = net -3. Not worth it without Str. Actually wait: Reaper deals 4 to each enemy and heals for total damage dealt = 8 healing. But I take 7 Thorns from... does Reaper trigger Thorns? Reaper is an attack that hits all enemies. Each enemy hit triggers their Thorns. So 2 enemies * 7 Thorns = 14 damage to me. 8 healing vs 14 Thorns damage = net -6. Terrible. OK plan: Play FNP(1E) for future exhaust value. Play Intimidate(0E, exhaust, Weak all, FNP triggers 3 block). Then I need to block 7 incoming. FNP gives 3 from Intimidate exhaust. Need 4 more block. No block cards in hand. Bash gives Vuln but costs 2E and triggers Thorns 7. Not worth it. So: FNP(1E) + Intimidate(0E exhaust, FNP 3 block) = 3 block. Plus Orichalcum: gives 6 block if block=0 at end of turn. But I already have 3 block from FNP, so Orichalcum wont trigger. 3 block vs 7 incoming (S2 Weakened: 7*0.75=5.25->5). Actually S2 still has Weak from Intimidate last turn? Let me check - S2 was Weakened 1 from T2 Intimidate. Weak decreases by 1 per turn. T2: applied. T3: decrements to 0. So S2 is NOT Weakened anymore. Wait the state shows no Weakened on either. Intent is 7 raw. If I play Intimidate now, it applies Weak 1. Then S2s 7 becomes 7*0.75=5.25->5. 3 FNP block vs 5 = 2 damage. 46-2=44 HP. Alternatively: Dont play FNP. Play Intimidate(0E exhaust). Then Orichalcum 6 block (block is 0 since no FNP). S2 Weakened: 5 incoming vs 6 block = zero damage! Thats better! But I lose FNP setup. Hmm. FNP is very valuable for the rest of this fight AND for future fights. 2 HP cost is worth it. Play FNP(1E) + Intimidate(0E, FNP 3 block) + end. Take 2 damage (5-3=2). OR: Skip FNP, play Intimidate(0E exhaust only, no FNP yet), Orichalcum 6 block, take 0 damage (5 Weakened vs 6 block). But then FNP not set up. I think the 2 HP is worth it for FNP. Actually for this hallway fight FNP is huge - I have Intimidate, Shockwave, Fiend Fire all exhausting. Each exhaust = 3 block. Lets play FNP + Intimidate. 3 block vs 5 Weakened = 2 damage. 44 HP. Then I have 4E left. Demon Form(3E) + 1E spare? In a hallway fight, Demon Form is too slow. Skip it. So 4E wasted. Just take the 2 HP hit and set up FNP for future turns.
